Alerts when hourly credit consumption by unit-of-measure deviates over 50% above the 7-day average baseline, using compare-with-timeshift on _view=credits_usage_hourly_v1, flagging abnormal burn rate for scheduled notification.

Alerts when the average daily credit burn rate from the credits_report lookup exceeds 95% or 100% of the account's annual plan, projecting estimated credits needed to reach contract end for scheduled daily notification.

Alerts when infrequent-tier search scan credits exceed per-query, per-user, or account-wide thresholds, computed from _view=sumologic_search_usage_per_query scanned-byte breakdowns excluding sumosupport, for scheduled cost-control notification.

Projects the predicted percent-of-plan credit consumption based on the average daily credit rate observed in the selected time window, computed from _view=credits_usage_hourly* summed by unit of measure.
Reports total units and credits consumed per unit-of-measure with daily rate and percent-of-plan, summarized from _view=credits_usage_hourly* for account-level cost review.
Charts total daily credits consumed over time from _view=credits_usage_hourly_v* with a smoothed trend line, referencing the account's annual credit plan for context.
Computes the average credits-per-gigabyte rate across CSE, Continuous, Infrequent and Frequent tiers from _view=credits_usage_hourly*, giving a normalized cost-efficiency metric.
Shows the proportion of total credits consumed by unit-of-measure as a pie chart from _view=credits_usage_hourly_v*, for a quick breakdown of where credit spend goes.
Charts daily credit consumption broken down by unit-of-measure over time from _view=credits_usage_hourly_v*, transposed into a stacked time series for trend visualization.
Charts daily credits by unit-of-measure over a user-defined lookback window, reading from a saved credits_report lookup table for long-term historical trend analysis beyond normal retention.
Reports total and average daily credits per unit-of-measure with percent-of-plan over a configurable lookback window, sourced from the saved credits_report lookup for long-term cost review.
Charts total daily credits over a configurable lookback window with a smoothed trend line, sourced from the saved credits_report lookup against the account's annual plan.
Shows the proportion of credits consumed by unit-of-measure as a pie chart over a configurable lookback window, sourced from the saved credits_report lookup.
Projects the predicted percent-of-plan credit consumption based on the average daily rate over a configurable lookback window, sourced from the saved credits_report lookup.

Estimates total credits needed to reach the contract anniversary date at the current burn rate, computed from the saved credits_report lookup and configurable plan parameters, for long-term budget forecasting.

Saves a daily-timesliced summary of credits, units and rate by unit-of-measure from _view=credits_usage_hourly_v1 into the credits_report lookup table, enabling dashboards to display history beyond normal search retention.

Computes hourly infrequent-tier scan credits from _view=sumologic_search_usage_per_query scanned-byte breakdowns excluding sumosupport users, producing a normalized credits/units row for the current search window.

Computes hourly infrequent-tier scan credits from _view=sumologic_search_usage_per_query and saves the result into the credits_usage_hourly_v1 view, backfilling historical credit usage data for the infrequent-scan unit of measure.

Computes hourly ingest credits per data tier by parsing _index=sumologic_volume view_and_tier_volume JSON and applying tier-specific credit rates, producing a normalized credits row for the current search window.

Computes hourly ingest credits per data tier from _index=sumologic_volume view_and_tier_volume and saves the result into the credits_usage_hourly_v1 view, backfilling historical log ingest credit usage.

Computes hourly metrics ingest credits by converting datapoints-per-minute from _index=sumologic_volume sourcecategory_metrics_volume into a normalized credits row, accounting for the 24-hour-average nature of DPM billing.

Computes hourly metrics ingest credits from datapoints-per-minute in _index=sumologic_volume sourcecategory_metrics_volume and saves the result into the credits_usage_hourly_v1 view, backfilling historical metrics credit usage.

Estimates hourly storage credits using fixed daily storage GB figures entered from the account page, applying tier-specific storage rates to produce a normalized credits row for storage cost tracking.
Estimates hourly storage credits using fixed daily storage GB figures and saves the result into the credits_usage_hourly_v1 view, backfilling historical storage credit usage estimates.
Computes hourly tracing ingest credits by converting billed bytes from _index=sumologic_volume sourcecategory_tracing_volume into a normalized credits row using the tracing credit rate.

Computes hourly tracing ingest credits from billed bytes in _index=sumologic_volume sourcecategory_tracing_volume and saves the result into the credits_usage_hourly_v1 view, backfilling historical tracing credit usage.

Lists the top 1000 credit-consuming combinations of data tier, collector, source category, source and index, computed from _index=sumologic_volume granular_volume with filters for tier/partition/collector/category, for granular cost attribution.

Lists distinct index and source-category combinations with event counts from granular usage data (_index=sumologic_volume granular_volume), building ready-to-click search-scope URLs to jump directly into that data.

Charts event volume over time grouped by a user-selected dimension (collector, source category, index, etc.) from granular usage data (_index=sumologic_volume granular_volume), transposed into a stacked time series.

Shows the top 25 contributors by gigabytes ingested grouped by a user-selected dimension as a pie chart, from granular usage data (_index=sumologic_volume granular_volume), for identifying the largest volume sources.
Charts the top-N hosts by a selected CPU metric using topk on the most recent metric value, letting users explore which hosts have the highest current CPU usage.
Charts the top-N hosts over time for a selected CPU metric, ranking by inverse latest value (100 minus latest) to surface hosts trending toward highest CPU utilization.
Charts the top-N hosts by a selected memory metric using topk on the most recent value, helping identify hosts under the greatest memory pressure.
Charts the top-N hosts and disks by average disk usage metric, grouped by host and disk name then ranked by latest value, to find the most disk-constrained volumes.
Displays a tabular view of a user-selected metric across all its dimensions, ranked by topk on the latest value, for flexible ad hoc host metric exploration.
Charts the top-N hosts for a second user-selectable custom metric using topk on the latest value, extending the dashboard's flexible top-N host metric exploration.
Charts the top-N hosts for a third user-selectable custom metric using topk on the latest value, providing another flexible slot for ad hoc host metric ranking.
Charts the top-N Windows hosts by a selected CPU metric using topk on the latest value, filtered with Telegraf Windows performance-counter fields for Windows-specific CPU monitoring.
Charts the bottom-N hosts and disks by average Percent_Free_Space using bottomk on the latest value, surfacing the disks closest to running out of free space.
Charts average per-process CPU usage from Telegraf procstat metrics grouped by host and process executable name, used as one input for comparing process CPU against total host CPU count.
Charts the average number of CPUs per host from the Telegraf host_system n_cpus metric, providing the denominator used to normalize per-process CPU usage against total host capacity.
Computes per-process CPU usage divided by host CPU count along host name, combining two prior panel series (#A / #B) to normalize process CPU load against total available host CPU capacity.
Charts average host CPU time spent in I/O wait state from the Telegraf host_cpu usage_iowait field grouped by host, highlighting hosts bottlenecked on disk or network I/O.
Charts average host CPU time spent in user space from the Telegraf host_cpu usage_user field grouped by host, one component of the overall CPU utilization breakdown.
Charts average host CPU time spent in system/kernel space from the Telegraf host_cpu usage_system field grouped by host, one component of the overall CPU utilization breakdown.
Charts average host CPU idle time from the Telegraf host_cpu usage_idle field grouped by host, used as a baseline for deriving total CPU utilization (100 minus idle).
Charts average host CPU time spent servicing software interrupts from the Telegraf host_cpu usage_softirq field grouped by host, another component of the CPU utilization breakdown.

Charts combined host CPU utilization by summing iowait, user, system and softirq component series (#C + #E + #F + #H) grouped by host name, giving a fuller picture of non-idle CPU usage than a single field alone.

Charts average host CPU time in software-interrupt state from the Telegraf host_cpu usage_softirq field grouped by host, feeding into the combined non-idle CPU utilization calculation on this dashboard.
Charts average host CPU idle percentage from the Telegraf host_cpu usage_idle field grouped by host, used as the basis for deriving total CPU utilization via a 100-minus-idle calculation.
Charts total host CPU utilization by computing 100 minus the idle-percentage series (100 - #C) along host name, giving a single non-idle CPU usage line per host from Telegraf metrics.
Charts a user-selected Telegraf IIS/ASP.NET metric filtered by a chosen field/value pair, letting the viewer swap in any of several Windows IIS performance metrics for ad hoc inspection.
Charts a second user-selected Telegraf IIS/ASP.NET metric filtered by a chosen field/value pair, giving the viewer a parallel line-chart panel for comparing another performance metric.
Charts a third user-selected Telegraf IIS/ASP.NET metric filtered by a chosen field/value pair, supporting flexible drill-down into additional IIS/ASP.NET performance counters over time.
Charts a fourth user-selected Telegraf IIS/ASP.NET metric scaled to a 0-100 range, filtered by a chosen field/value pair, useful for percentage-style utilization metrics.
Charts a fifth user-selected Telegraf IIS/ASP.NET metric filtered by a chosen field/value pair, extending the dashboard's flexible metric-exploration panels for Windows web servers.
Displays a sixth user-selected Telegraf IIS/ASP.NET metric as a stacked column chart filtered by a chosen field/value pair, for comparing metric contributions across the selected scope.
Charts CPU seconds consumed by Sumo Logic otelcol collector pods per cluster/namespace, converting the cumulative counter to a rate via quantize/delta, to monitor collector resource health.
Charts resident memory (RSS) usage of Sumo Logic otelcol collector pods per cluster/namespace, averaged then summed per pod, to track memory consumption trends for collection health.
Counts otelcol_otelsvc_k8s_pod_deleted events for a namespace to show how many collection pods were deleted, a churn indicator on the Kubernetes collection-health dashboard.

Charts exporter queue size over time per pod for Sumo Logic collection pods from otelcol_exporter_queue_size, averaged by container/pod, to detect backpressure or export bottlenecks in the collection pipeline.
Counts otelcol_otelsvc_k8s_pod_added events for a namespace to show how many collection pods were added, a churn indicator on the Kubernetes collection-health dashboard.
Charts the count of up=1 targets scraped for the kube-controller-manager job in a cluster, quantized to 2 minutes and summed, as a control-plane availability signal.
Charts the count of up=1 targets scraped for DNS-related jobs in a cluster, quantized to 2 minutes and summed, as a cluster-DNS availability signal.
Charts the count of up=1 targets scraped for the apiserver job in a cluster, quantized to 2 minutes and summed, as an API-server availability signal.
Charts the count of up=1 targets scraped for the kubelet job in a cluster, quantized to 2 minutes and summed, as a kubelet availability signal.
Charts the count of up=1 targets scraped for the kube-state-metrics job in a cluster, quantized to 2 minutes and summed, as a metrics-exporter availability signal.
Charts the count of up=1 targets scraped for the node-exporter job in a cluster, quantized to 2 minutes and summed, as a node-metrics-exporter availability signal.
Displays a single-value summary of Prometheus scrape target uptime for jobs matching 'prom*' in the selected cluster, quantized and summed, to confirm Prometheus exporters are up.
Charts the rate of failed remote-write sample deliveries from Prometheus remote storage in the selected cluster, highlighting metric-shipping failures that need troubleshooting.
Charts the rate of successfully delivered remote-write samples from Prometheus remote storage in the selected cluster, used as a healthy-throughput baseline for metric shipping.
Charts the rate of samples pending in the Prometheus remote-write queue across all clusters, an early indicator of backpressure or delivery lag before failures occur.
Lists scrape-target up status summed per job, instance and endpoint in the selected cluster, ranked with topk, to quickly spot which Prometheus targets are down.
Charts the replica count of the otelcol-logs statefulset in the selected cluster/namespace over time, to verify the expected number of logs-collector pods are running.
Charts working-set memory usage per pod for the otelcol-logs statefulset in the selected cluster/namespace, averaged over time, to monitor memory pressure on log collectors.
Charts CPU usage per pod for the otelcol-logs statefulset in the selected cluster/namespace, converting the cumulative counter to a rate and averaging, to monitor collector CPU load.
Charts the rate of CPU throttling events for otelcol-logs pods in the selected cluster/namespace, flagging pods being CPU-limited by their resource quotas.
Charts the exporter queue size for the otelcol metadata-logs service per pod in the selected cluster/namespace, averaged over time, to detect export backlog building up.
Tracks 502 Bad Gateway and 503 Service Unavailable errors from otelcol-logs statefulset logs over time by cluster, parsing the HTTP error code and pivoting counts into a time series table.
Charts the replica count of the otelcol-metrics statefulset in the selected cluster/namespace over time, verifying the expected number of metrics-collector pods are running.
Charts working-set memory usage per pod for the otelcol-metrics statefulset in the selected cluster/namespace, averaged then summed, to monitor memory consumption of metrics collectors.
Charts CPU usage per pod for the otelcol-metrics statefulset in the selected cluster/namespace, converting the counter to a rate then averaging and summing, to track collector CPU load.
Charts the exporter queue size per pod for the otelcol-metrics statefulset in the selected cluster/namespace, averaged and summed, to spot metrics export backlog.
Tracks 502 Bad Gateway errors emitted by otelcol-metrics statefulset pods over time in the selected cluster/namespace, pivoting per-pod error counts into a time series table.
Charts the rate of CPU throttling events for otelcol-metrics statefulset pods in the selected cluster/namespace, identifying metrics collectors constrained by CPU limits.

Charts memory usage (mislabeled as spans sent) per pod across the cluster's OpenTelemetry collection pods from otelcol_process_memory_rss, averaged by container/pod, as a collection-health capacity signal.

Lists otelcol log lines indicating retry exhaustion, send failures, dropped data or full queues, parsing embedded JSON error/kind/pipeline fields and ranking by frequency for troubleshooting collector reliability.
Charts otelcol error/warning/fatal log events related to queue retries, failures or full queues over time, grouped and pivoted by error type, to visualize collector reliability trends.

Lists non-Normal Kubernetes events for otelcol/prometheus/falco/otel-agent/otel-gateway components in the selected cluster/namespace, parsing JSON event fields to surface warnings and errors needing attention.
Charts non-Normal Kubernetes events for collector-related workloads over time by reason, pivoting counts into a time series table to reveal spikes in abnormal cluster events.

Lists non-chatty otelcol log events other than known error patterns, parsing structured JSON fields for error/message/code/name and aggregating by level and prefix, for general collector troubleshooting.
Charts non-chatty otelcol container log events over time grouped by event prefix, pivoted into a time series table to spot changes in the volume of collector log activity.
Charts the count of failed log-record sends by the otelcol metadata-logs exporter per pod in the selected cluster/namespace, summed over time, to flag delivery failures.
Charts the rate of successfully sent log records by the otelcol metadata-logs exporter per pod, derived from a cumulative counter via quantize/delta, to confirm healthy log export throughput.
Charts the rate of bytes sent in export requests by the otelcol metadata-logs exporter per pod, derived via quantize/delta, to monitor outbound log export volume.
Charts the exporter queue capacity for the otelcol metadata-logs service per pod, averaged over time, forming the denominator for a queue-usage-percentage calculation.
Computes queue usage percentage (size divided by capacity times 100) for the otelcol metadata-logs exporter per pod, combining two prior metric queries to flag queue saturation risk.
Charts otelcol non-info-only log events over time broken out by log level, pivoting counts into a time series table to visualize warning/error/fatal trends for the collector.
Charts the rate of CPU throttling events for otelcol daemonset pods in the selected cluster/namespace, identifying agent-level collectors constrained by CPU limits.
Tracks 502 Bad Gateway and 503 Service Unavailable errors from otelcol-logs daemonset logs over time by cluster, parsing the HTTP error code and pivoting counts into a time series table.
Charts the exporter queue size for otelcol daemonset pods in the selected cluster/namespace, averaged per pod over time, to detect export backlog at the agent level.
Charts the count of failed log-record sends by otelcol daemonset pods in the selected cluster/namespace, summed over time, to flag agent-level log delivery failures.
Charts the exporter queue size for otelcol daemonset pods, averaged over time, serving as the numerator input for a queue-usage-percentage calculation at the agent level.
Charts the exporter queue capacity for otelcol daemonset pods, averaged over time, serving as the denominator input for a queue-usage-percentage calculation at the agent level.
Computes queue usage percentage (size divided by capacity times 100) for otelcol daemonset pods, combining two prior metric queries to flag agent-level queue saturation risk.
Charts the count of running otelcol-logs-collector pods in the selected cluster/namespace over time, summed per pod/service, to confirm expected collector replica count.
Charts working-set memory usage per pod for the sumo-otelcol-logs-collector service in the selected cluster/namespace, averaged over time, to monitor log-collector memory consumption.
Charts CPU usage per pod for the sumo-otelcol-logs-collector service in the selected cluster/namespace, converting the cumulative counter to a rate and averaging, to monitor collector CPU load.
Displays a single-value count of Ready nodes in the selected cluster, quantized and summed with a small timeshift, as a top-level cluster health indicator.
Displays a single-value count of nodes not in Ready condition in the selected cluster, summed after quantization, flagging cluster nodes needing attention.
Displays a single-value count of pods in Running phase in the selected cluster, summed after quantization, as a cluster-wide capacity/health indicator.
Displays a single-value count of pods in Failed phase in the selected cluster, summed after quantization, flagging pods that need troubleshooting.
Displays cluster-wide CPU utilization percentage derived from node idle-time metrics (100 minus idle rate), as a single-value summary of overall compute usage.
Displays per-node CPU utilization percentage as a honeycomb heatmap, derived from idle-time rate and ranked with topk, to spot individual nodes running hot.
Scales the per-node CPU usage series from the prior panel by 100 and displays it broken out along node, rendering the percent-CPU-per-node honeycomb visualization.
Displays cluster-wide memory utilization percentage computed from two prior available/total memory metric queries, as a single-value summary of overall memory pressure.
Charts total available memory bytes summed across nodes in the selected cluster, quantized over time, feeding the memory-usage-percentage calculation.
Charts total memory capacity in bytes summed across nodes in the selected cluster, quantized over time, feeding the memory-usage-percentage calculation.
Displays cluster-wide disk space usage percentage computed from two prior available/total filesystem metric queries, summarizing overall storage pressure across nodes.
Charts available filesystem bytes per node (excluding tmpfs) in the selected cluster, quantized over time, feeding the disk-space-usage-percentage calculation.
Charts total filesystem size in bytes per node (excluding tmpfs) in the selected cluster, quantized over time, feeding the disk-space-usage-percentage calculation.
Displays cluster-wide network throughput (receive plus transmit bytes, excluding veth) as a rate summed across nodes, quantized to a single value, indicating overall network load.
Displays the cluster-wide rate of dropped network packets (receive and transmit, excluding veth) summed across nodes, quantized to a single value, flagging network issues.
Computes the percentage of network traffic dropped relative to total traffic by dividing two prior metric queries, summarizing network health as a single value.
Displays the total count of CPU cores across all nodes in the selected cluster, counted by node/cpu label combination and summed, as a capacity-planning reference value.
Displays total cluster memory capacity in megabytes, converting bytes and summing across nodes, as a capacity-planning reference value.
Displays a single-value count of pods that are neither Running nor Pending (i.e. unschedulable/failed) in the selected cluster, flagging scheduling problems.

Lists pods currently pending, unknown, or failed from kube_pod_status_phase, summed by pod and namespace and filtered to nonzero, to pinpoint which specific pods are unschedulable in a honeycomb view.
Summarizes the most recent 100 Kubernetes cluster events by reason and involved-object kind, parsing JSON event fields from the events log source to surface recent cluster activity and issues.
Charts total allocatable CPU cores summed across the selected cluster over time, one of three series compared against actual usage and requested CPU for capacity planning.
Charts total CPU cores requested by pod containers summed across the selected cluster over time, compared against allocatable capacity and actual usage for capacity planning.
Charts actual CPU usage rate summed across the selected cluster over time, compared against allocatable capacity and requested CPU to assess CPU capacity headroom.
Labels the allocatable-CPU series from panel A as "Allocatable" for display in the combined CPU usage-versus-capacity chart.
Labels the CPU-requests series from panel B as "Requests" for display in the combined CPU usage-versus-capacity chart.
Labels the CPU-usage series from panel C as "Usage" for display in the combined CPU usage-versus-capacity chart.
Charts total memory capacity in megabytes summed across the selected cluster's nodes over time, one of three series compared against requested and actual memory usage.
Charts total memory requested by pod containers in megabytes summed across the selected cluster over time, compared against capacity and actual usage for capacity planning.
Charts actual working-set memory usage in megabytes summed across the selected cluster over time, compared against capacity and requests to assess memory headroom.
Labels the memory-usage series from panel C as "Usage" for display in the combined memory allocatable-versus-requests-versus-usage chart.
Charts the daily count of Kubernetes cluster events over time using a 1-day timeslice on the events log source, to visualize event volume trends for a cluster.
Lists the most recent 100 log messages for a cluster, parsing the JSON "log" field and sorting by message time descending, to give administrators a quick view of raw cluster log output.
Lists the most recent 100 error or critical log messages for a cluster, parsing the JSON "log" field and sorting by message time descending, to surface recent problems needing attention.
Displays a single-value count of currently scheduled daemonset pods in the selected cluster, maxed per cluster and summed, as a quick daemonset health check.
Charts the current number of scheduled daemonset pods per cluster over time, one of two series compared against desired count to spot daemonset scheduling gaps.
Charts the desired number of scheduled daemonset pods per cluster over time, compared against the current scheduled count to spot daemonset scheduling gaps.
Charts desired statefulset replica counts as a column chart in the selected cluster over time, compared against actual current replicas to identify under-provisioned statefulsets.
Charts current statefulset replica counts as a column chart in the selected cluster over time, compared against desired replicas to identify under-provisioned statefulsets.
Displays a single-value count of distinct namespaces observed via container CPU metrics in the selected cluster, giving a quick view of cluster tenancy scope.
Displays a single-value sum of statefulset replica counts across the selected cluster, quantized over time, as a workload-inventory summary metric.
Charts inbound network traffic in megabytes per second summed across the selected cluster, derived as a rate and ranked with topk, to monitor cluster ingress bandwidth.
Charts outbound network traffic in megabytes per second summed across the selected cluster, derived as a rate and ranked with topk, to monitor cluster egress bandwidth.
Displays failed pods as a honeycomb heatmap per pod in the selected cluster, filtered to only nonzero counts, to visually pinpoint which specific pods are failing.
Charts the count of pods whose containers are not reporting ready from kube_pod_container_status_ready in the kubernetes origin, filtered to a latest value under 1, as a not-ready-pods single value.
Displays not-ready pods as a honeycomb heatmap per pod/namespace in the selected cluster, filtering to pods whose readiness status is below 1, to flag unhealthy workloads.
Displays a single-value count of distinct nodes reporting status conditions in the selected cluster, as a quick cluster-size/node-inventory indicator.
Displays a single-value count of distinct nodes in the selected cluster with a small timeshift, giving an at-a-glance node inventory on the cluster overview dashboard.
Displays a single-value count of Ready nodes in the selected cluster, summed after quantization with a small timeshift, as a top-level cluster health indicator on the overview dashboard.
Displays a single-value sum of node-ready condition status in the selected cluster, quantized over time, as an alternate node-health rollup for the overview dashboard.
Displays a single-value count of pods in Running phase in the selected cluster with a small timeshift, as a top-level workload health indicator on the overview dashboard.
Displays a single-value count of pods in Failed phase in the selected cluster with a small timeshift, flagging pods needing troubleshooting on the overview dashboard.
Charts total available memory bytes summed across nodes in the selected cluster, quantized over time, feeding a memory-usage summary on the overview dashboard.
Charts total memory capacity in bytes summed across nodes in the selected cluster, quantized over time, feeding a memory-usage summary on the overview dashboard.
Displays cluster-wide disk space usage percentage computed from two prior filesystem metric queries, summarizing overall storage pressure on the overview dashboard.
Charts total filesystem size in bytes per node (excluding tmpfs) in the selected cluster, quantized over time, feeding the disk-space-usage-percentage calculation on the overview dashboard.
Displays cluster-wide network throughput (receive plus transmit bytes, excluding veth) as a rate summed across nodes, quantized to a single value, on the overview dashboard.
Displays total cluster memory capacity in megabytes, converting bytes and summing across nodes, as a capacity reference value on the overview dashboard.
Charts the number of nodes reporting node-info metrics over time for the selected cluster, summed per cluster, to visualize node count trends and detect scale-up/down events.
Displays a single-value count of pods in pending, unknown or failed phase in the selected cluster, summed after quantization, flagging unschedulable or unhealthy pods.
Charts total pod count over time for the selected cluster, summed per pod then per cluster from container-info metrics, to visualize workload scaling trends.
Displays the top 5 pods by CPU usage rate as a honeycomb heatmap for the selected cluster, ranked with topk, to quickly identify the heaviest CPU consumers.
Displays the top 5 pods by memory usage in megabytes as a honeycomb heatmap for the selected cluster, ranked with topk, to quickly identify the heaviest memory consumers.
Displays a single-value count of distinct namespaces observed via container CPU metrics in the selected cluster, giving a quick tenancy-scope view on the overview dashboard.
Displays a single-value sum of pod status-phase metrics across the selected cluster, quantized over time, as an overall pod-count rollup on the overview dashboard.
Retrieves available memory per node from node_memory_MemAvailable_bytes for a cluster, feeding a percent-resource-usage honeycomb that lets users explore node-level memory pressure interactively.
Retrieves total memory per node from node_memory_MemTotal_bytes for a cluster, feeding a percent-resource-usage honeycomb that lets users explore node-level memory capacity interactively.
Displays per-node, per-metric resource usage percentage as a honeycomb heatmap, computed from two prior queries and ranked with topk, letting users explore CPU/memory hotspots across nodes.

Computes per-node CPU usage percentage by inverting idle CPU time from node_cpu_seconds_total, rate-converted and minimum-aggregated by node, scaled to percent and ranked by topk in a resource-usage honeycomb.

Lets a user drill into relative CPU usage (in cores) for a chosen pod across nodes from container_cpu_usage_seconds_total, rate-converted and maxed by node/pod, ranked by topk in a honeycomb for pod-level troubleshooting.
Displays relative memory usage in megabytes per pod as a honeycomb heatmap, filtered by cluster and a user-selected pod, ranked with topk, to compare memory consumption across nodes.
Charts CPU usage rate per pod for the selected Sumo Logic collector override deployment in a cluster/namespace, averaged and summed, to monitor collector CPU health.
Charts resident memory (RSS) usage per pod for the otelcol collector in the selected cluster/namespace, averaged then summed, to monitor collector memory consumption.
Charts exporter queue size per pod for the otelcol collector in the selected cluster/namespace, averaged then summed, to detect export backlog building up.

Displays a single-value summary of scrape target uptime for the kube-controller-manager job in the selected cluster, summed after quantization, confirming the control-plane component is being monitored.
Displays a single-value summary of scrape target uptime for DNS-related jobs in the selected cluster, summed after quantization, confirming cluster DNS components are being monitored.
Displays a single-value summary of scrape target uptime for the apiserver job in the selected cluster, summed after quantization, confirming the Kubernetes API server is being monitored.
Displays a single-value summary of scrape target uptime for the kubelet job in the selected cluster, summed after quantization, confirming node kubelets are being monitored.
Displays a single-value summary of scrape target uptime for the kube-state-metrics job in the selected cluster, summed after quantization, confirming that metrics source is healthy.
Displays a single-value summary of scrape target uptime for the node-exporter job in the selected cluster, summed after quantization, confirming node-level metrics collection is healthy.
Lists Prometheus scrape target up status summed per job and endpoint in the selected cluster, ranked with topk, to quickly identify which monitoring targets are down.
Charts the replica count of the Sumo Logic override otelcol-logs statefulset in the selected cluster/namespace over time, verifying expected log-collector pod count.

Counts Kubernetes liveness-probe-failed events over time for logs-collector pods (excluding the collector itself) by parsing event messages from the cluster events source, timesliced to 1 minute, to flag collection instability.

Counts Kubernetes readiness-probe-failed events over time for logs-collector pods (excluding the collector itself) by parsing event messages from the cluster events source, timesliced to 1 minute, to flag collection instability.
Charts working-set memory usage per pod for the Sumo Logic override otelcol-logs statefulset in the selected cluster/namespace, averaged then summed, to monitor log-collector memory.

Charts CPU usage per pod for the Sumo Logic override otelcol-logs statefulset in the selected cluster/namespace, converting the counter to a rate then averaging and summing, to monitor collector CPU load.

Charts CPU throttling over time per pod for the logs-collection statefulset from container_cpu_cfs_throttled_seconds_total, rate-converted, to spot workloads being CPU-limited — a Kubernetes collection-health capacity signal.
Charts exporter queue size per pod for the Sumo Logic override otelcol-logs statefulset in the selected cluster/namespace, averaged then summed, to detect log-export backlog.

Counts occurrences of "502 Bad Gateway" log messages over time per pod for the logs-collection statefulset, timesliced to 5 minutes and transposed into a pod-by-time matrix, to detect collector connectivity errors.
Charts the replica count of the Sumo Logic override otelcol-metrics statefulset in the selected cluster/namespace over time, verifying expected metrics-collector pod count.
Charts working-set memory usage per pod for the Sumo Logic override otelcol-metrics statefulset in the selected cluster/namespace, averaged then summed, to monitor metrics-collector memory.

Charts CPU usage per pod for the Sumo Logic override otelcol-metrics statefulset in the selected cluster/namespace, converting the counter to a rate then averaging and summing, to monitor collector CPU load.
Counts Kubernetes liveness-probe-failed events over time for metrics-collector pods by parsing event messages from the cluster events source, timesliced to 1 minute, to flag collection instability.
Counts Kubernetes readiness-probe-failed events over time for metrics-collector pods by parsing event messages from the cluster events source, timesliced to 1 minute, to flag collection instability.
Charts exporter queue size per pod for the Sumo Logic override otelcol-metrics statefulset in the selected cluster/namespace, averaged then summed, to detect metrics-export backlog.

Counts occurrences of "502 Bad Gateway" log messages over time per pod for the metrics-collection statefulset, timesliced to 5 minutes and transposed into a pod-by-time matrix, to detect collector connectivity errors.

Charts the rate of memory RSS usage per pod for the otelcol traces/spans collector in the selected cluster, averaged then summed, used as a proxy for spans processing load and collector health.

Charts whether a specific container is stuck in CrashLoopBackOff by quantizing the kube_pod_container_status_waiting_reason metric filtered to that reason, letting operators pinpoint restart-loop failures for a chosen namespace/cluster/pod/container.

Lists detailed container identity and placement info (node, image, container ID) from the kube_pod_container_info metric, summed and ranked with topk for a filtered namespace/cluster/pod/container selection.
Charts container memory requests in MB over time by converting kube_pod_container_resource_requests bytes and summing per pod/cluster/container, helping size memory reservations against usage.

Charts actual container memory usage in MB over time from container_memory_working_set_bytes, summed per pod/cluster/container, to compare against configured requests and limits.

Labels the prior memory-limits series as 'Limits' so the combined chart clearly distinguishes the configured memory limit from usage and requests on the same panel.
Charts CPU core requests over time from kube_pod_container_resource_requests for a filtered container/pod, summed per pod/cluster/container to compare against actual CPU usage and limits.
Charts CPU core limits over time from kube_pod_container_resource_limits for a filtered container/pod, summed per pod/cluster/container to compare against usage and requests.
Charts actual CPU core consumption over time by rating container_cpu_usage_seconds_total and summing per pod/cluster/container, for comparison against configured requests and limits.
Displays the configured memory limit for a specific container as a single value, quantizing kube_pod_container_resource_limits to compute percent-of-limit consumption alongside actual usage.
Displays current container memory usage as a single value from container_memory_working_set_bytes, used alongside the limit metric to compute percent of memory limit consumed.

Displays the configured CPU core limit for a specific container as a single value from kube_pod_container_resource_limits, used to compute percent of CPU limit consumed.

Charts the daily count of Kubernetes Pod events for a specific pod over time by parsing the *events* index JSON payload for namespace/name/kind and timeslicing by day, to spot event bursts.
Tracks container readiness state over time from kube_pod_container_status_ready, taking the max per container/pod/cluster and ranking the top 500 to spot containers failing readiness checks.
Lists the latest 100 Kubernetes Pod events for a specific pod, JSON-parsing the *events* index for involvedObject kind/name and the event message/timestamp, sorted most-recent first.
Charts the CoreDNS cache hit rate over time by rating coredns_cache_hits_total and summing per cluster, used alongside cache misses to gauge DNS caching efficiency.
Charts the CoreDNS cache miss rate over time by rating coredns_cache_misses_total and summing per cluster, paired with cache hits to compute overall cache hit percentage.

Charts cumulative CoreDNS request duration (seconds) over time from coredns_dns_request_duration_seconds_sum, used with the request count metric to derive average DNS query latency.
Charts the count of CoreDNS request duration samples over time from coredns_dns_request_duration_seconds_count, paired with the duration sum to compute average query latency.
Computes average CoreDNS DNS request latency per pod by dividing cumulative duration by request count, highlighting pods experiencing slow DNS resolution.
Tracks cumulative CPU time consumed by the CoreDNS process over time from process_cpu_seconds_total, summed per cluster to monitor CoreDNS resource consumption trends.
Breaks down CoreDNS request volume by network protocol (UDP/TCP) over time, rating coredns_dns_requests_total and summing per protocol to spot shifts in transport usage.
Shows the distribution of CoreDNS cache entries by cache type as a pie chart, summing coredns_cache_entries per type to understand cache composition and sizing.
Tracks the number of open file descriptors held by the CoreDNS process over time from process_open_fds, summed per cluster to detect file-descriptor leaks or exhaustion risk.
Charts CoreDNS process memory usage in GB over time by converting process_resident_memory_bytes and summing per cluster, useful for spotting memory growth or leaks.

Displays the count of misscheduled DaemonSet pods as a single value from kube_daemonset_status_number_misscheduled, flagging pods running on nodes they shouldn't be scheduled on.
Displays the count of unavailable DaemonSet pods as a single value from kube_daemonset_status_number_unavailable, highlighting DaemonSets not meeting availability targets.
Charts the current number of scheduled DaemonSet pods over time from kube_daemonset_status_current_number_scheduled, compared against desired count to visualize rollout progress.

Ranks the top 5 DaemonSets by memory consumption in MB using a honeycomb visualization, converting and summing container_memory_working_set_bytes per DaemonSet.
Lists the latest 100 error log lines for a DaemonSet's pods by JSON-parsing the docker log field, filtering non-null messages, and sorting by message time descending for troubleshooting.
Lists the latest 100 raw log lines for a DaemonSet's pods by JSON-parsing the docker log field and sorting by message time descending, for general log inspection.
Charts the number of pods belonging to a DaemonSet over time by counting distinct pod/cluster combinations from kube_pod_container_info, useful for tracking scale changes.

Charts the number of pods in a Deployment over time by counting distinct pods from kube_pod_container_info per cluster, revealing scale-up/scale-down history.

Charts actual CPU core usage over time for a Deployment by rating container_cpu_usage_seconds_total and summing per cluster/deployment, for comparison against requests and limits.
Labels the actual CPU usage series as 'Usage' so the combined chart clearly distinguishes real consumption from requested and limit values on the same panel.
Charts actual memory usage in MB over time for a Deployment by converting container_memory_working_set_bytes and summing per cluster/deployment, to compare against requests and limits.

Labels the actual memory usage series as 'Usage' so the combined chart clearly distinguishes real consumption from requested and limit values on the same panel.

Charts total CPU core limits for all pods on a node over time from kube_pod_container_resource_limits, summed to compare against node CPU capacity and requests.

Charts over time which nodes are not in Ready condition by summing kube_node_status_condition grouped by condition and node, helping spot node outages across the cluster.

Charts containers stuck in a waiting state grouped by reason (excluding normal ContainerCreating), using kube_pod_container_status_waiting_reason summed over time to surface abnormal pod startup issues.

Charts terminated containers grouped by termination reason over time using kube_pod_container_status_terminated_reason, helping identify unexpected pod/container exits for a given cluster/namespace/pod.

Flags whether a pod is currently in CrashLoopBackOff by filtering kube_pod_container_status_waiting_reason for that reason and summing, giving a single-value crash-loop indicator for the selected pod.
Lists the latest 100 error or critical log lines from a selected pod, parsing the JSON log field and sorting by message time descending for rapid troubleshooting.
Displays the ready/not-ready state of pods over time using kube_pod_container_status_ready, quantized to max and ranked with topk, to visualize pod availability trends for the selected pod/cluster.
Lists the latest 100 log lines from a selected pod, parsing the JSON log field and sorting by message time descending for general log review.
Charts container CPU usage as a rate of container_cpu_usage_seconds_total, averaged by container/pod and summed, to feed a percent-of-CPU-limit gauge for the selected pod.
Reports the configured CPU limit (kube_pod_container_resource_limits, resource=cpu) for the selected pod's containers, summed after quantizing, to complete the percent-of-CPU-limit calculation.
Computes the percentage of CPU limit currently consumed by a pod by dividing usage (#A) by limit (#B) and scaling to 100, shown as a single-value stat.
Charts actual CPU usage in cores over time (rate of container_cpu_usage_seconds_total, max aggregated) for the selected pod, plotted alongside limits and requests to spot CPU pressure.
Charts the configured CPU resource limit over time (kube_pod_container_resource_limits, resource=cpu, min aggregated) for the selected pod, for comparison against actual usage and requests.
Charts the configured CPU resource request over time (kube_pod_container_resource_requests, resource=cpu, min aggregated) for the selected pod, for comparison against usage and limits.
Relabels the prior CPU requests series (#C) as 'Requests' for display in the CPU usage-vs-limits-vs-requests comparison chart for the selected pod.
Charts container memory usage (container_memory_working_set_bytes) averaged by container/pod and summed, to feed a percent-of-memory-limit gauge for the selected pod.

Reports the configured memory limit (kube_pod_container_resource_limits, resource=memory) for the selected pod's containers, summed after quantizing, to complete the percent-of-memory-limit calculation.
Charts container memory usage in MB over time (container_memory_working_set_bytes converted from bytes, max aggregated) for the selected pod, for comparison against limits and requests.
Charts the configured memory resource limit in MB over time (kube_pod_container_resource_limits, resource=memory, min aggregated) for the selected pod, for comparison against usage and requests.
Charts the configured memory resource request in MB over time (kube_pod_container_resource_requests, resource=memory, min aggregated) for the selected pod, for comparison against usage and limits.
Reports current CPU usage for a pod's containers as a single-value stat by rating container_cpu_usage_seconds_total, averaging per container, and summing across the pod.
Reports current memory usage in MB for the selected pod by averaging container_memory_working_set_bytes per container/pod and summing, as a single-value memory usage indicator.
Lists pods with their container ready-state counts using kube_pod_container_status_ready, summed by pod/cluster and ranked with topk, as a table of running containers per pod.
Lists the latest 100 Kubernetes events for a selected pod by parsing namespace, involved object name/kind, timestamp, and message fields from the events JSON, sorted by timestamp descending.
Reports the current pod restart count by taking the delta of kube_pod_container_status_restarts_total (max quantized), surfacing recent container restarts for troubleshooting stability issues.
Counts Kubernetes event-log entries whose involvedObject is the selected pod, parsing JSON fields and bucketing by day to show a timeline of pod-related cluster events.
Lists pod metadata — service, namespace, deployment, daemonset, statefulset, and node — from kube_pod_info, giving an inventory table for the selected pod.
Ranks the top 10 CPU-consuming containers using the rate of container_cpu_usage_seconds_total summed by cluster/pod/container, visualized as a honeycomb for capacity troubleshooting.
Ranks the top 10 memory-consuming containers in MB using container_memory_working_set_bytes summed by cluster/pod/container, visualized as a honeycomb to spot memory-heavy workloads.
Charts incoming network traffic in MB per pod as the rate of container_network_receive_bytes_total, summed by pod and ranked by topk, for network capacity monitoring.
Charts outgoing network traffic in MB per pod as the rate of container_network_transmit_bytes_total, summed by pod and ranked by topk, for network capacity monitoring.
Flags pods in a non-running state (pending, unknown, or failed) using kube_pod_status_phase, summed after max quantizing, as an abnormal-pod-status indicator for troubleshooting.
Reports the count of containers currently in a waiting state using kube_pod_container_status_waiting_reason, summed after max quantizing, contributing to an overall pod-status health panel.
Reports the count of containers currently in a terminated state using kube_pod_container_status_terminated_reason, summed after max quantizing, contributing to an overall pod-status health panel.
Combines pending/failed phase, waiting, and terminated container signals (#A+#B+#C) into a single pod-status health indicator to quickly flag any abnormal pod state.
Counts total Falco security events logged across the cluster by parsing the log.priority JSON field, shown as a single-value stat for overall security-alert volume.
Counts Falco security events with Critical priority by parsing the log.priority JSON field and filtering, shown as a single-value stat highlighting the most severe alerts.
Counts Falco security events with Emergency priority by parsing the log.priority JSON field and filtering, shown as a single-value stat for the highest-severity runtime threats.
Counts Falco security events with Alert priority by parsing the log.priority JSON field and filtering, shown as a single-value stat tracking elevated-severity findings.
Counts Falco security events with Error priority by parsing the log.priority JSON field and filtering, shown as a single-value stat tracking lower-severity anomalies.
Breaks down all Falco security events by priority level (Emergency, Critical, Alert, Error, etc.) as a pie chart, showing the severity distribution of triggered rules across the cluster.
Ranks the top 10 most frequently triggered Falco security rules by parsing the log.rule JSON field and counting occurrences, shown as a pie chart to spot the noisiest detections.
Reports which Kubernetes namespaces have the most Falco rule triggers by parsing the k8s.ns.name output field and counting, sorted into a table to localize security noise by namespace.
Reports which pods have the most Falco rule triggers by counting events grouped by pod, sorted into a table to identify which workloads are generating the most security alerts.
Breaks down Falco rule triggers by the user.name output field (defaulting to 'unknown' when blank) as a bar chart, showing which users are associated with triggered security events.
Breaks down Falco rule triggers by the offending process name (proc.name, defaulting to 'none' when blank) as a bar chart, surfacing which executables are triggering runtime security rules.
Breaks down Falco rule triggers by the parent process name (proc.pname, defaulting to 'none' when blank) as a column chart, revealing the process lineage behind detected security events.
Charts a timeline of Emergency, Alert, Critical, and Error Falco events in 5-minute buckets, transposed into separate series per priority to visualize security-alert trends over time.
Charts the overall volume of Falco security events over time in 5-minute buckets as an area chart, giving a baseline trend of runtime security activity across the cluster.
Counts Falco security events where the rule field equals 'Modify Shell Configuration File', parsed from JSON log output, to alert on unauthorized shell profile tampering in a Kubernetes cluster.
Lists all Falco security rule detections in a cluster with process, user, pod, namespace and command-line context, parsed from JSON, to investigate which runtime security rules fired and by whom.
Counts Falco alerts matching the 'System procs network activity' rule to flag system processes unexpectedly making network connections inside a Kubernetes cluster.
Counts Falco alerts for the 'Non sudo setuid' rule, identifying processes that changed user/group ID without sudo, a common privilege-escalation indicator in the cluster.
Counts Falco alerts for the 'DB program spawned process' rule, flagging database processes that unexpectedly spawned child processes, a potential compromise signal in the cluster.
Counts Falco alerts for the 'Write below etc' rule, tracking unauthorized writes to /etc configuration paths that could indicate tampering with system configuration in the cluster.
Counts Falco alerts for the 'Run shell untrusted' rule, flagging shell processes spawned by untrusted parent programs as a possible container breakout or malware indicator.
Counts Falco alerts for the 'Read sensitive file untrusted' rule, tracking reads of sensitive files (like credentials) by untrusted processes across the Kubernetes cluster.
Counts Falco alerts for the 'Modify binary dirs' rule, flagging attempts to alter executables in system binary directories, a sign of possible malware persistence.
Counts Falco alerts for the 'Write below binary dir' rule, tracking writes to protected binary directories that may indicate an attacker planting malicious executables.
Counts Falco alerts for the 'Change thread namespace' rule, flagging processes that altered Linux namespaces, a technique often used for container escape attempts.
Counts Falco alerts for the 'Create files below dev' rule, tracking suspicious file creation under /dev that can indicate device-node abuse or privilege escalation attempts.
Counts Falco alerts for the 'Write below rpm database' rule, flagging tampering with the RPM package database that could hide unauthorized software installs on cluster nodes.
Counts Falco alerts for the 'Mkdir binary dirs' rule, tracking creation of new directories under system binary paths as a possible sign of malware staging.
Counts Falco alerts for the 'System user interactive' rule, flagging interactive shell logins by system accounts that normally should never log in interactively.
Reports the number of pods in Running phase for the selected service using kube_pod_status_phase, summed by cluster/pod, as a service-health indicator on the Kubernetes Service dashboard.
Reports the total pod count for the selected service using kube_pod_container_info, counted by cluster/pod, giving an overall pod inventory figure for the service.
Breaks down error log volume by service for a selected cluster/namespace/service to identify which service is generating the most errors, ranked as a pie chart of the top 10.
Tracks the number of pods backing a service over time using kube_pod_container_info, summed by pod and counted by service, to visualize service scaling trends.
Charts the delta (increase) of container restarts for a service's pods using kube_pod_container_status_restarts_total, summed by cluster, to correlate restart spikes with pod running counts.
Charts pod counts over time for the selected service using kube_pod_container_info, summed by pod and counted by cluster, paired with restart deltas to spot stability issues.
Charts CPU resource requests in cores for a service's pods using kube_pod_container_resource_requests (resource=cpu), summed by cluster/service, for comparison against usage and limits.
Charts CPU resource limits in cores for a service's pods using kube_pod_container_resource_limits (resource=cpu), summed by cluster/service, for comparison against usage and requests.
Charts actual CPU usage in cores for a service's pods as the rate of container_cpu_usage_seconds_total, summed by cluster/service, for comparison against requests and limits.
Charts memory usage in MB for a service's pods using container_memory_working_set_bytes converted from bytes, summed by cluster/service, for comparison against requests and limits.
Charts memory resource requests in MB for a service's pods using kube_pod_container_resource_requests (resource=memory), summed by cluster/service, for comparison against usage and limits.
Charts memory resource limits in MB for a service's pods using kube_pod_container_resource_limits (resource=memory), summed by cluster/service, for comparison against usage and requests.
Reports the number of pods in Failed phase for the selected service using kube_pod_status_phase, summed by cluster/pod, flagging service-level pod failures.
Reports the count of terminated (non-completed) containers for the selected service using kube_pod_container_status_terminated_reason, summed, flagging abnormal container exits.
Reports the count of containers in a waiting state for the selected service using kube_pod_container_status_waiting_reason, summed, flagging pods stuck starting up.
Lists up to 500 pods with their ready-container counts for the selected service using kube_pod_container_status_ready, summed by pod/namespace/service, as a service pod inventory table.
Ranks the top 10 CPU-consuming pods within a service using the rate of container_cpu_usage_seconds_total summed by cluster/pod, shown as a honeycomb for capacity troubleshooting.
Ranks the top 10 memory-consuming pods within a service in MB using container_memory_working_set_bytes summed by cluster/pod, shown as a honeycomb to spot memory-heavy pods.
Lists the latest 100 error log messages for pods in a selected cluster/namespace/service, extracting the log field from JSON and deduplicating by message and service for quick triage.
Lists the latest 100 log messages (not limited to errors) for pods in a selected cluster/namespace/service, parsing the log field from JSON for general log review.
Reports the total current CPU usage rate for a service, summed across all pods, from container_cpu_usage_seconds_total, giving a single-value capacity snapshot.
Lists service metadata by counting container_cpu_usage_seconds_total series per cluster/service, effectively an inventory table of monitored services and their sample counts.
Reports current memory usage in MB for the selected service by averaging container_memory_working_set_bytes per container/pod/service and summing, as a single-value service memory indicator.
Charts incoming network traffic in MB for a service as the rate of container_network_receive_bytes_total, summed by service and ranked by topk, for network capacity monitoring.
Charts outgoing network traffic in MB for a service as the rate of container_network_transmit_bytes_total, summed by service and ranked by topk, for network capacity monitoring.
Reports the desired replica count for the selected StatefulSet using kube_statefulset_replicas, max aggregated, as a baseline for the StatefulSet overview dashboard.
Reports the StatefulSet's metadata generation number using kube_statefulset_metadata_generation, max aggregated, used to detect whether a spec update has been applied.
Reports the StatefulSet's observed generation using kube_statefulset_status_observed_generation, max aggregated, compared against metadata generation to detect stalled rollouts.
Charts the desired replica count for the StatefulSet over time using kube_statefulset_replicas, max aggregated, plotted against current replicas to spot scaling mismatches.
Charts the current replica count for the StatefulSet over time using kube_statefulset_status_replicas, max aggregated, plotted against desired replicas to spot scaling mismatches.
Reports the total current CPU usage rate for a StatefulSet, summed across pods, from container_cpu_usage_seconds_total, as a single-value capacity snapshot.
Reports current memory usage in MB for the StatefulSet's pods by averaging container_memory_working_set_bytes per container/pod and summing, as a single-value memory indicator.
Tracks the number of pods backing the StatefulSet over time using kube_pod_container_info, summed by pod and counted, to visualize StatefulSet scaling trends.
Ranks the top 5 CPU-consuming StatefulSets using the rate of container_cpu_usage_seconds_total summed by statefulset, shown as a honeycomb for capacity troubleshooting.
Ranks the top 5 memory-consuming StatefulSets in MB using container_memory_working_set_bytes summed by statefulset, shown as a honeycomb to spot memory-heavy StatefulSets.
Lists the latest 100 error or critical log messages for a StatefulSet's pods, extracting the log field from JSON and deduplicating by message for quick triage.
Lists the latest 100 log messages (not limited to errors) for a StatefulSet's pods, parsing the log field from JSON for general log review.
Charts the daily count of Kubernetes events involving a selected StatefulSet name, parsed from the events source JSON, to spot spikes in cluster-reported activity or issues.
Charts the count of Kubernetes nodes reporting a Ready condition for a cluster, quantized to 30s and summed, as a single-value node-health indicator on the cluster dashboard.
Charts the count of Kubernetes nodes not reporting Ready status for a cluster, quantized using max and summed, to flag nodes needing attention.
Charts the total count of pods in the Running phase for a cluster from kube_pod_status_phase, quantized to 1 minute using max, as a capacity/health single value.
Charts the total count of pods in the Failed phase for a cluster from kube_pod_status_phase, quantized to 1 minute using max, to flag failing workloads.
Charts overall cluster CPU usage percentage by inverting idle CPU time from node_cpu_seconds_total, using rate and average across nodes, quantized to 1 minute, as a top-level capacity indicator.
Charts CPU usage percentage per node by inverting idle CPU time from node_cpu_seconds_total, rate-converted and averaged by node, to compare relative node load in a honeycomb view.
Charts overall cluster memory usage percentage by comparing available versus total memory metrics, averaged and scaled to percent, quantized to 1 minute, as a top-level capacity indicator.
Charts available memory summed across a cluster from node_memory_MemAvailable_bytes, quantized to 1 minute, feeding the memory-usage percentage calculation.
Charts total memory summed across a cluster from node_memory_MemTotal_bytes, quantized to 1 minute, feeding the memory-usage percentage calculation.
Charts overall cluster disk space usage percentage by comparing free versus total filesystem bytes, averaged and scaled to percent, quantized to 1 minute, as a top-level capacity indicator.
Charts available disk space per node (excluding tmpfs) from node_filesystem_avail_bytes, summed by node, feeding the disk-space-usage percentage calculation.
Charts total disk space size per node (excluding tmpfs) from node_filesystem_size_bytes, summed by node, feeding the disk-space-usage percentage calculation.

Charts total network throughput across a cluster by summing receive and transmit byte-rate metrics (excluding veth interfaces), quantized to 1 minute, feeding the dropped-traffic percentage calculation.
Charts dropped network packets across a cluster by summing receive and transmit drop-total metrics (excluding veth interfaces), rate-converted and quantized to 1 minute, to detect network issues.
Computes the percentage of dropped network traffic by dividing dropped packet rate by total traffic rate from the two prior panels, quantized to 1 minute, as a cluster network-health indicator.
Charts the total number of CPU cores available across a cluster's nodes from node_cpu_seconds_total, counted by node and cpu then summed, as a capacity-planning baseline.
Charts total physical memory (in MB) available across a cluster's nodes from node_memory_MemTotal_bytes, quantized to 1 minute using max and summed, as a capacity-planning baseline.
Charts the count of pods stuck in a phase other than Running or Pending from kube_pod_status_phase, quantized to 1 minute using max and summed, to flag unschedulable workloads.
Charts allocatable CPU cores for a cluster from kube_node_status_allocatable, quantized to 1 minute and summed, as one series in a CPU usage-versus-capacity comparison chart.

Charts total CPU core requests declared by pod containers for a cluster from kube_pod_container_resource_requests, quantized to 1 minute and summed, as one series in a CPU usage-versus-capacity comparison chart.
Charts actual CPU usage rate for a cluster from container_cpu_usage_seconds_total, quantized to 1 minute and summed, as one series in a CPU usage-versus-capacity comparison chart.
Charts allocatable memory (in MB) for a cluster from kube_node_status_capacity, quantized to 1 minute and summed, as one series in a memory allocatable-versus-requests-versus-usage comparison chart.
Charts total memory requests (in MB) declared by pod containers for a cluster from kube_pod_container_resource_requests, quantized to 1 minute and summed, as one series in a memory comparison chart.
Charts actual memory usage (in MB) for a cluster from container_memory_working_set_bytes, quantized to 1 minute and summed, as one series in a memory comparison chart.
Charts the current number of daemonset pods scheduled for a cluster from kube_daemonset_status_current_number_scheduled, quantized to 1 minute using max and summed, as a capacity single value.
Charts the current number of daemonset pods scheduled per cluster from kube_daemonset_status_current_number_scheduled over time, to compare against the desired count.
Charts the desired number of daemonset pods scheduled per cluster from kube_daemonset_status_desired_number_scheduled over time, to compare against the current count and detect scheduling gaps.

Charts the configured replica count for statefulsets in a cluster from kube_statefulset_replicas, quantized to 1 minute using max, as the desired-replicas series in a desired-versus-current comparison.

Charts the actual running replica count for statefulsets in a cluster from kube_statefulset_status_replicas, quantized to 1 minute using max, as the current-replicas series in a desired-versus-current comparison.
Charts the count of distinct namespaces actively reporting CPU usage in a cluster from container_cpu_usage_seconds_total, quantized to 1 minute, as a workload-scope indicator.
Charts the total statefulset replica count running in a cluster from kube_statefulset_status_replicas, quantized to 1 minute using max and summed, as a capacity single value.
Charts inbound network throughput (in MB) for a cluster from container_network_receive_bytes_total, rate-converted, summed, and scaled, showing the top series by peak value over time.
Charts outbound network throughput (in MB) for a cluster from container_network_transmit_bytes_total, rate-converted, summed, and scaled, showing the top series by peak value over time.
Lists which specific pods are in the Failed phase for a cluster from kube_pod_status_phase, quantized to 30s, summed by pod, and filtered to nonzero, in a honeycomb view for quick triage.
Lists which specific pods and namespaces have containers not reporting ready from kube_pod_container_status_ready, filtered to a latest value under 1, in a honeycomb view for quick triage.
Charts the total count of Kubernetes nodes reporting a status condition for a cluster from kube_node_status_condition, quantized to 1 minute using max, as a node-inventory single value.
Charts the total count of Kubernetes nodes reporting a status condition for a cluster from kube_node_status_condition, quantized to 30s using max, as an overview node-inventory single value.
Charts the count of nodes reporting a Ready condition for a cluster, quantized to 30s and summed, as an overview node-health single value.
Charts the count of nodes reporting ready status for a cluster from kube_node_status_condition, quantized to 1 minute using max and summed, as an overview node-health single value.
Charts the total count of pods in the Running phase for a cluster from kube_pod_status_phase, quantized to 30s and summed, as an overview capacity single value.
Charts the total count of pods in the Failed phase for a cluster from kube_pod_status_phase, quantized to 30s and summed, as an overview troubleshooting single value.
Charts overall cluster disk space usage percentage by comparing free versus total filesystem bytes from prior panels, averaged and scaled to percent, quantized to 1 minute, for the cluster overview.
Charts dropped network packets across a cluster by summing receive and transmit drop metrics (excluding veth interfaces), rate-converted and quantized to 1 minute, for the cluster overview.
Charts total physical memory (in MB) available across a cluster's nodes from node_memory_MemTotal_bytes, quantized to 1 minute using max and summed, for the cluster overview.
Charts the number of nodes reporting kube_node_info for a cluster over time, quantized to 1 minute and summed, to visualize cluster node-count trends.
Charts the count of pods stuck pending, unknown, or failed from kube_pod_status_phase for a cluster, summed, to flag unschedulable workloads on the overview dashboard.
Charts the number of running pods per cluster over time from kube_pod_container_info, quantized to 1 minute using average and summed by pod then by cluster, for the cluster overview trend.
Lists the top 5 pods by CPU usage for a cluster from container_cpu_usage_seconds_total, rate-converted, summed by pod, and ranked with topk, in a honeycomb view for spotting CPU-hungry workloads.
Lists the top 5 pods by memory usage (in MB) for a cluster from container_memory_working_set_bytes, summed by pod and ranked with topk, in a honeycomb view for spotting memory-hungry workloads.
Charts the count of distinct namespaces actively reporting CPU usage in a cluster from container_cpu_usage_seconds_total, quantized to 1 minute, as an overview workload-scope indicator.
Charts the total count of pods in any phase for a cluster from kube_pod_status_phase, quantized to 1 minute using max and summed, as an overview pod-inventory single value.

Computes per-node percent memory usage by comparing available versus total memory from prior panels, taking the max per cluster/node/metric and showing the top 500 nodes scaled to percent in a honeycomb.

Lets a user drill into relative memory usage (in MB) for a chosen pod across nodes from container_memory_working_set_bytes, quantized to 1 minute and maxed by node/pod, ranked by topk in a honeycomb for pod-level troubleshooting.

Charts CPU usage over time per pod for the Sumo Logic OpenTelemetry logs-collection pods from otelcol_process_cpu_seconds, rate-converted and averaged by container/pod, as a collection-health capacity signal.
Charts memory usage over time per pod for the Sumo Logic OpenTelemetry collection pods from otelcol_process_memory_rss, averaged by container/pod, as a collection-health capacity signal.
Lists scrape-target up status by job and endpoint for a cluster from the up metric, quantized to 2 minutes, summed, and ranked by topk, to give a consolidated collection-health status table.
Charts the replica count of the Sumo Logic OpenTelemetry logs-collection statefulset from kube_statefulset_status_replicas, quantized using max, to track logs-collector pod-count health over time.
Charts memory usage over time per pod for the logs-collection statefulset from container_memory_working_set_bytes, averaged by container/pod, as a collection-health capacity signal.
Charts CPU usage over time per pod for the logs-collection statefulset from container_cpu_usage_seconds_total, rate-converted and averaged by container/pod, as a collection-health capacity signal.

Charts exporter queue size over time per pod for the logs-collection statefulset from otelcol_exporter_queue_size, averaged by container/pod, to detect export backpressure in the logs-collection pipeline.

Charts the replica count of the Sumo Logic OpenTelemetry metrics-collection statefulset from kube_statefulset_status_replicas, quantized using max, to track metrics-collector pod-count health over time.
Charts memory usage over time per pod for the metrics-collection statefulset from container_memory_working_set_bytes, averaged by container/pod, as a collection-health capacity signal.
Charts CPU usage over time per pod for the metrics-collection statefulset from container_cpu_usage_seconds_total, rate-converted and averaged by container/pod, as a collection-health capacity signal.

Charts exporter queue size over time per pod for the metrics-collection statefulset from otelcol_exporter_queue_size, averaged by container/pod, to detect export backpressure in the metrics-collection pipeline.
Flags whether a container is stuck in CrashLoopBackOff by reading kube_pod_container_status_waiting_reason for that reason, quantized and summed over 5-minute windows for troubleshooting.
Lists detailed container identity info (node, image, container ID, pod, cluster) from kube_pod_container_info metric, grouped and topk-ranked, for inventory and troubleshooting lookups.
Charts a container's requested memory in MB over time from kube_pod_container_resource_requests, converting bytes to MB and grouping by pod/cluster/container for capacity comparison.
Charts a container's memory limit in MB over time from kube_pod_container_resource_limits, converting bytes to MB and grouping by pod/cluster/container to compare against usage and requests.
Charts actual container memory usage in MB over time from container_memory_working_set_bytes, converted from bytes and grouped by pod/cluster/container, to compare against requests and limits.
Charts a container's requested CPU cores over time from kube_pod_container_resource_requests (resource=cpu), grouped by pod/cluster/container, for capacity comparison against usage and limits.
Charts a container's CPU core limit over time from kube_pod_container_resource_limits (resource=cpu), grouped by pod/cluster/container, to compare against requested and actual CPU usage.
Charts actual container CPU usage in cores over time by rating container_cpu_usage_seconds_total, grouped by pod/cluster/container, to compare against requested cores and configured limits.
Reports the configured memory limit for a container from kube_pod_container_resource_limits (resource=memory), quantized to 1-minute max, as the denominator for a percent-of-limit calculation.
Reports actual container memory usage from container_memory_working_set_bytes, quantized to 1-minute max, as the numerator feeding a percent-of-memory-limit calculation.
Reports the configured CPU core limit for a container from kube_pod_container_resource_limits (resource=cpu), quantized to 1-minute max, as the denominator for a percent-of-limit calculation.
Reports actual container CPU usage by rating container_cpu_usage_seconds_total, quantized to 1-minute max, as the numerator feeding a percent-of-CPU-limit calculation.
Charts the container readiness state over time from kube_pod_container_status_ready, grouped by container/pod/cluster and topk-ranked, to see when a container flips ready/not-ready.
Charts the rate of CoreDNS cache hits per cluster over time from coredns_cache_hits_total, feeding a cache-hit-percentage calculation used to gauge DNS caching effectiveness.
Charts the rate of CoreDNS cache misses per cluster over time from coredns_cache_misses_total, feeding a cache-hit-percentage calculation used to gauge DNS caching effectiveness.
Charts the rate of CoreDNS DNS responses grouped by response code (rcode) from coredns_dns_responses_total, useful for spotting spikes in DNS errors like SERVFAIL or NXDOMAIN.
Shows the proportion of DNS requests CoreDNS forwards to each upstream server from coredns_forward_requests_total, grouped by destination, to understand upstream DNS dependency distribution.
Charts the cumulative sum of CoreDNS request duration in seconds from coredns_dns_request_duration_seconds_sum, quantized per minute, as the numerator for computing average DNS latency.
Charts the count of CoreDNS requests from coredns_dns_request_duration_seconds_count, quantized per minute, as the denominator for computing average DNS latency.
Charts total CPU seconds consumed by the CoreDNS process over time from process_cpu_seconds_total (job=coredns), summed per minute, as a capacity/resource-usage signal for the DNS service.
Charts the rate of CoreDNS DNS requests grouped by transport protocol (proto, e.g. UDP vs TCP) from coredns_dns_requests_total, useful for understanding query traffic composition.
Shows the breakdown of CoreDNS cache entry counts by cache type from coredns_cache_entries, summed per minute, to understand cache memory footprint composition.
Charts the number of open file descriptors held by the CoreDNS process over time from process_open_fds (job=coredns), summed per minute, as a resource-exhaustion capacity signal.
Charts CoreDNS process resident memory in GB over time from process_resident_memory_bytes (job=coredns), converted from bytes and summed per minute, as a memory-capacity signal for the DNS service.
Reports the desired number of scheduled pods for a DaemonSet from kube_daemonset_status_desired_number_scheduled, quantized to 5 minutes and summed per cluster, for capacity/rollout tracking.
Reports the current number of scheduled pods for a DaemonSet from kube_daemonset_status_current_number_scheduled, quantized to 5 minutes and summed per cluster, to track rollout progress.
Flags DaemonSet pods that were scheduled onto nodes they shouldn't run on, using kube_daemonset_status_number_misscheduled quantized to 5 minutes and summed, to catch scheduling misconfiguration.
Flags nodes where a DaemonSet's pod should be running but is unavailable, using kube_daemonset_status_number_unavailable quantized to 5 minutes and summed, to catch rollout or health problems.
Charts the current number of scheduled DaemonSet pods per cluster over time from kube_daemonset_status_current_number_scheduled, for comparison against the desired count on the same chart.
Charts the desired number of scheduled DaemonSet pods per cluster over time from kube_daemonset_status_desired_number_scheduled, for comparison against the current count on the same chart.
